chapters.title: 1. What this demo teaches
chapters.summary: The recording explains how ServiceNow Service Catalog work is implemented in Seismic, the UI Framework used for Next Experience pages and builders.

chapters.title: 2. Web Components foundation
chapters.summary: Seismic builds reusable UI units using web component concepts. The demo highlights three important web-component ideas.

chapters.title: 3. Anatomy of a Seismic component
chapters.summary: A UI Framework component is created with createCustomElement from @seismic/core. It receives two key arguments.

chapters.title: 4. View function
chapters.summary: The view function returns the markup that Seismic renders into the DOM.

chapters.title: 5. Properties and state pipeline
chapters.summary: Properties are the mechanism for passing data into components. State combines property values, defaults, persisted values and computed values before rendering.

chapters.title: 6. Actions and action handlers
chapters.summary: Actions are the eventing mechanism for notifying the component, ancestor components, or the application that something happened.

chapters.title: 7. Lifecycle actions
chapters.summary: The framework emits lifecycle actions to expose major component lifecycle moments.

chapters.title: 8. Documentation and developer workflow
chapters.summary: After the concept slides, the demo switches into documentation, source code, and runtime inspection.

chapters.title: 9. Service Catalog demo flow
chapters.summary: The demo validates Seismic behavior in Service Catalog authoring screens.

chapters.title: 10. Code review section
chapters.summary: The final section of the demo returns to implementation details in the codebase.
